sections.heading: Find Atlas in Azure
sections.body:
If you are an Azure (Microsoft Azure) user with any role, you can discover Atlas through the following methods:

- Search for MongoDB Atlas using the search bar in the Azure (Microsoft Azure) dashboard and view Atlas
  under **Services** and **Marketplace** in the search results.

  ![Azure search bar image for navigating MongoDB Atlas](/reference/images/azure-native-isv-service-searchbar.png)

- Navigate to **All services**, then to **Databases**, from which you can select
  **MongoDB Atlas**.

  ![Azure databases page for navigating MongoDB Atlas](/reference/images/azure-native-isv-service-databases-page.png)

- Navigate to **Marketplace**, then to **Databases**, from which you can select
  **MongoDB Atlas**.

sections.heading: Create an Atlas Organization as an Azure Native Integration
sections.body:
You can provision Atlas as an Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native Integration within Azure (Microsoft Azure) by filling
out a **Create a MongoDB Atlas Organization** form, specifying the organization
name, the Azure (Microsoft Azure) resource to which it will be linked, and billing plan details.

See the [Overview of Azure Native Integrations](https://aka.ms/ANI/MongoDBAtlas/Docs)
to learn more.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
- You have an Owner or a Contributor role in the Azure (Microsoft Azure) subscription.

- The Atlas organization name that you input meets Atlas naming conventions:
  organization names can't be longer than 64 characters and can only contain letters, numbers,
  spaces, and the following symbols: (), @, &, +. :, ., \_, -, ', ,.

After Azure (Microsoft Azure) generates an Atlas resource, Atlas will:

- Create an Atlas organization.

- Grant the user the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role in Atlas. This sends the user an
  invitation to join the Atlas organization and configure their account.

- Link billing of the Atlas organization to the Azure (Microsoft Azure) marketplace billing offer.

sections.heading: Supported Regions
sections.body:
The Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native Integration supports the  **US East** region only for creating
organizations. To deploy an organization in another region, create your deployment
directly through Atlas rather than through the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native Integration.

sections.heading: Billing
sections.body:
Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native Integration creates new Atlas Orgs from Azure (Microsoft Azure) which are automatically connected
to an Azure (Microsoft Azure) marketplace billing plan for the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native Integration.

In Azure (Microsoft Azure) and Atlas, you will see confirmation of Azure (Microsoft Azure) marketplace billing for the Atlas
organization.

sections.heading: Create Your Atlas User Account
sections.body:
When the Atlas organization is provisioned, Atlas creates an account for the email address of the
Azure (Microsoft Azure) user who initiated provisioning. The user account that Atlas creates uses the email address
configured in the user's Azure (Microsoft Azure) account. If you are a new user, you will need to configure your Atlas
user account by setting your password. You can access Atlas to configure your account in the following
ways:

- You see a confirmation in Azure (Microsoft Azure) that the Atlas organization is successfully created.

- You see a `Go to Atlas` link in Azure (Microsoft Azure), and receive a `Get started with your new Atlas Organization`
  email with this link.

- You click the link and goes to Atlas. If you are a new user, you are prompted to set your password.

- You see your organization as the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>).

> **Note:** If your email domain is already registered for SSO (Single Sign-On) with Atlas, you don't have to set a password.

Once your new organization is created, MongoDB recommends that you:

- Invite your teammates to Atlas to collaborate.

- Set up SSO (Single Sign-On) with Microsoft Entra ID. See [Microsoft Entra ID](federated-auth-azure-ad) to learn more.

- Create clusters.

> **Note:** Atlas organizations created through Azure Native Integration can host clusters only on Azure (Microsoft Azure).

sections.heading: View Your Organization in Azure and Atlas
sections.body:
You can view and manage your organization in the MongoDB Atlas portal by clicking on the **Go to MongoDB Atlas**
portal URL on the **MongoDB Resource Overview** page.

![Azure Atlas Organization Portal URL](/reference/images/azure-atlas-organization-portal-url.png)

Alternatively, you can access the organization directly from your Atlas dashboard.

sections.heading: Unlink Your Organization from Azure
sections.body:
If you are an Azure (Microsoft Azure) Subscription Owner or a Contributor, you can unlink your MongoDB Atlas instance from
Azure (Microsoft Azure), which unlinks the Atlas organization from Azure Native Integration and Azure (Microsoft Azure) marketplace billing,
but doesn't delete the organization in Atlas.

If paid clusters exist on the organization when Azure (Microsoft Azure) billing is removed, users will need to provide a payment
method within Atlas to avoid the suspension or termination of services.
